headline: BaseballPro77 Just Broke the League — And Everyone Else Is Scrambling
subheadline: Nineteen home runs, 39 RBI, a 2.33 ERA, and a 7-2 demolition later, the most dangerous team in BeerLeagueBaseball just put the rest of the league on notice.
body:
## Thriller of the Week

### Canberra Wombats vs. Riverside Roughnecks — 4-4 Tie

You know that moment in a playoff game when two teams are so evenly matched that neither one can land the knockout punch, and by the fourth quarter everyone's just sort of exhausted and vaguely disappointed? That was the **Canberra Wombats** and **Riverside Roughnecks** this week. A dead-even 4-4 tie where neither team could separate in pitching or hitting — the Wombats put up Jo Adell (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Pete Crow-Armstrong providing some life on the bases, while the Roughnecks countered with Misiorowski's 14 strikeouts and a .368 OBP from a lineup that takes walks like it's getting paid by the base. The problem is that walks don't win fantasy categories by themselves (I checked), and 5 HR with a 4.55 ERA is basically begging your opponent to find you.

The real gut-punch here is the context. Both teams needed a full win desperately — the Wombats to climb out of the lower tier, the Roughnecks to make a push toward respectability — and instead they split the points and shuffled back to the middle of the standings together, like two guys who drove to the same destination and both got there 45 minutes late. A tie in fantasy baseball feels like the rom-com ending where nobody actually gets the girl. Maddening for both fanbases heading into a pivotal Week 5.

---

## The Week's Defining Moment

Look, every few weeks in a fantasy baseball season, a team puts together a performance so absurd, so comprehensively dominant, that you just have to set down your coffee and acknowledge what happened. Week 4 belonged entirely to **BaseballPro77**, and it wasn't close. Nineteen home runs. Thirty-nine RBI. A 2.33 ERA. A 7-2 demolition of the **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** that wasn't just a win — it was a statement, the kind of performance that makes every other manager in the league quietly log into their roster at 11pm on a Sunday and wonder if they need to make moves.

The offense was the headliner, obviously. Trout went absolutely nuclear with 5 HR, Murakami chipped in 4 of his own, and Riley added 3 more, meaning just those three players accounted for 12 of the team's record-setting 19 home runs. The Reapers, bless their hearts, managed 33 hits against 187 at-bats (a .257 OBP that reads like a typo) and 8 home runs of their own, but when your opponent is essentially cosplaying as the 1927 Yankees for a week, the outcome was never really in doubt. The final box score looked like the Reapers accidentally scheduled a warm-up game against a Triple-A affiliate and got the opponent wrong.

Here's the thing that makes this genuinely frightening for the rest of the league: the pitching kept pace. A 2.33 ERA with Williams and Kirby leading the staff, 71 strikeouts, 69.2 innings pitched — this wasn't just an offense-carried week. This was a complete, top-to-bottom performance, the fantasy equivalent of a team shooting 60% from the field while also holding the other team to 85 points. **BaseballPro77** rounds into form at Week 4 with a matchup against the **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** on deck, which, honestly, is must-watch fantasy television. You've been warned.

---

## Matchup Deep-Dives

### BaseballPro77 def. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers, 7-2

Already covered above in loving, extensive detail — but the numbers deserve one more look. The Reapers put up 8 HR and a 5.01 ERA, which in most weeks wins you a few categories and keeps things competitive. Against **BaseballPro77** this week, that was like showing up to a gunfight with a very confident handshake. The Reapers' decision to drop McGreevy without a real replacement continues to look concerning, and a .257 OBP offense is not a "down week" problem — it's a roster-construction problem. Week 5 brings a matchup against the **Albuquerque Bombers**, who are aggressively waiver-hunting, and that's not an easy draw for a team that needs to rediscover its offense.

### General Hospital def. Benchwarmers, 8-2

This was not competitive, and the box score confirms it with the enthusiasm of a disappointed parent. **General Hospital** put up 6 quality starts, a 2.68 ERA, a frankly absurd 0.96 WHIP, and Will Warren delivering 17 K with a 1.69 ERA — all while posting 10 HR and 36 RBI on offense. The **Benchwarmers**, meanwhile, got Michael King's 0.82 ERA as a consolation prize in what was otherwise a 4 HR, 4.40 ERA, 2-quality-start disaster. The 8-2 final isn't even the worrying part for the Benchwarmers — it's that their luck delta is the highest positive number in the league, meaning their actual record is significantly better than their production deserves. That's a bill that comes due eventually. **General Hospital**'s pitching staff with Kumar Rocker adding depth might be the best in the league, full stop.

### Athletic Park Dragons def. North Correa, 5-2

Aaron Judge doing Aaron Judge things — 5 HR, 7 RBI — while Bailey Ober contributes 17 strikeouts and the rotation posts a clean 3.01 ERA with 5 quality starts. The **Athletic Park Dragons** are quietly assembling one of the more complete rosters in the league, and this 5-2 win over **North Correa** confirmed that the rotation is healthy enough to actually complement the elite offense. **North Correa**, for their part, had 5 quality starts themselves and matched the Dragons in stolen bases (8 each), but a 5.56 ERA is a rotation problem that Ohtani's stolen-base upside and Nico Hoerner's 11 RBI can't paper over forever. Only 2 category wins in this matchup tells you where the gap is. The pitching needs a real fix before the playoff picture comes into focus.

### Albuquerque Bombers def. Sugar Land Skeeters, 5-4

This one was genuinely close, which doesn't show up enough in the headlines. The **Sugar Land Skeeters** posted 72 strikeouts and 4 quality starts — Ryan Weathers with an 18 K, 1.05 WHIP week is legitimately ace-level stuff — and still lost because a 6.05 ERA in your own pitching line is an anvil tied around your ankle in ERA and WHIP categories. **Albuquerque Bombers** got Byron Buxton going (3 HR, 8 R) and Ivan Herrera adding 2 HR, ran aggressive waiver activity all week, and scratched out a 5-4 win that felt more like controlled chaos than dominance. That's fine. A win is a win. The Skeeters remain dangerous — four weeks of elite offense don't lie — but fixing that ERA situation is the offseason project that needed to start three weeks ago.

### Abbotsford-Colby Twins def. Hiroshima Radiation, 6-4

The most infuriating loss of the week for the neutral observer. **Hiroshima Radiation** posted a 2.81 team ERA, had Skubal and Eovaldi both under 1.50 ERA individually, and lost 6-4 because of a -1 NSVH and an offense with 6 HR and 1 stolen base that simply cannot generate enough counting category wins. The **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** rode José Ramírez (4 HR, 5 SB, .533 OBP — read that OBP again, slowly) and Yordan Alvarez (4 HR, 7 RBI) to carry the offensive categories and did just enough everywhere else. The Twins win despite a 5.60 ERA and only 2 quality starts, which is the kind of thing that gives you a win today but creates problems tomorrow. For the Radiation, the -0.61-ish luck situation on their opponent's side notwithstanding, this team needs bats badly or they're going to keep posting beautiful ERA numbers in a losing cause.

### Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs def. Buttville Skidmarks, 6-3

Eight quality starts. *Eight.* That's the **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** rotation doing things this week, with Paul Skenes posting a 0.90 ERA and a 0.50 WHIP that looks like it was generated by a video game on rookie difficulty. Imanaga and Valdez round out what is legitimately the deepest rotation in BeerLeagueBaseball, and the 2.49 team ERA, 0.98 WHIP combination absolutely buried the **Buttville Skidmarks**, who had Junior Caminero (3 HR) and Bryan Reynolds (.500 OBP, 7 RBI) doing real work but watched MacKenzie Gore's 6.52 ERA and Gausman's 1.55 WHIP eat the pitching categories alive. The Skidmarks have legitimate offensive weapons — this isn't a talent-free roster — but until they fix the back end of the rotation, every week is going to feel like plugging holes in a sinking boat with increasingly creative materials.

---

## Lucky/Unlucky Team of the Week

**Lucky Team: Benchwarmers** — The **Benchwarmers** have a +1.21 luck delta, which is the highest positive number in this league right now, and I need everyone to understand what that means: their actual wins are dramatically, almost comically outpacing what their production deserves. Four home runs, a 4.40 ERA, and only 2 quality starts this week — among the weakest individual stat lines of any team — and yet the wins are there on the record. This is the fantasy equivalent of a poker player who's been catching cards all night but hasn't actually improved their fundamentals. The math catches up. It always catches up. (Ask anyone who's ever run a bluff for four straight weeks in a home game.)

**Unlucky Team: Abbotsford-Colby Twins** — The **Twins** won their matchup 6-4 and still carry the worst luck delta in the league at -0.61. José Ramírez with a .533 OBP, 4 HR and 5 stolen bases. Yordan Alvarez with 4 HR and 7 RBI. Fourteen team home runs, 8 stolen bases — and their strong category production is consistently generating fewer wins than any reasonable production model would project. The talent is genuinely elite. The 1-2 punch of Ramírez and Alvarez is as good as any in this league. But the luck number is a real concern, and a Week 5 matchup against the suddenly scorching **BaseballPro77** is not the schedule break they needed.

---

## League Records

**BaseballPro77** set two new single-week records in Week 4: most home runs by a team in a single week with 19 (a new league high), and most RBI by a team in a single week with 39 — both new BeerLeagueBaseball records. It's worth noting that both marks were set in the same week, against the same opponent, in the same demolition job. Records usually fall one at a time. Setting two in a single matchup is the kind of thing you talk about in the group chat for the next month. The plaque, wherever you hang league plaques, should read: *Week 4. BaseballPro77. The Reapers were there too.*

---

## Manager Spotlight: North Correa

**North Correa** is the most interesting puzzle box in BeerLeagueBaseball right now, and not entirely in a good way. Sitting at the 8-spot in this week's power rankings, the roster has genuine blue-chip talent — Ohtani providing stolen-base upside that very few teams in this league can match, Nico Hoerner quietly becoming one of the most reliable RBI sources week-to-week (11 this week alone), and a 5 quality-start week that shows real rotation depth on paper.

The problem — and it is a real problem — is a 5.56 ERA that has now shown up as a recurring theme. When your pitching is getting hammered in ERA and WHIP on a consistent basis, no amount of stolen bases and Hoerner clutch production fully compensates. The **Athletic Park Dragons** exposed this 5-2, winning the pitching categories handily and taking the matchup despite **North Correa** matching them in both stolen bases and quality starts. That's a hard loss to absorb strategically.

The playoff outlook is genuinely uncertain. The talent suggests this team *should* be fighting for a top-six spot. The ERA numbers suggest this team is one bad pitching week away from a genuine spiral. If there's a path forward, it runs directly through the trade market or the waiver wire for rotation help — because the offense and the speed game are already there. The question is whether management pulls the trigger before Week 5 or 6 makes the decision for them.

---

## Trade Value Watch

**José Ramírez (Abbotsford-Colby Twins):** Four home runs, five stolen bases, and a .533 OBP in a single week — Ramírez is doing what Ramírez always does, which is be an absolutely unhinged fantasy asset in every counting category simultaneously. **HOLD.** You don't sell a player performing at this level; you build around him.

**Paul Skenes (Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs):** A 0.90 ERA and a 0.50 WHIP as part of an eight-quality-start week for his team tells you everything about what Skenes is becoming in this league. He's not just good — he's the engine of what might be the best rotation in BeerLeagueBaseball. **BUY** if you can get him. **HOLD** if you have him.

**MacKenzie Gore (Buttville Skidmarks):** A 6.52 ERA in a week where your team lost 6-3 is not a one-week anomaly at this point — it's a trend. **SELL** while some other manager still believes the underlying talent overrides the results. Someone will bite.

---

## Power Rankings

1. **BaseballPro77** | Record-setting 19 HR and 39 RBI with a 2.33 ERA — this is the most complete performance of the season and the hottest team in the league right now [UP]
2. **Sugar Land Skeeters** | Lost the matchup but still put up 72 K and 4 QS; Ryan Weathers (18 K, 1.05 WHIP) is an ace-level performer and this offense remains elite over four weeks [SAME]
3. **General Hospital** | An 8-2 blowout win driven by 6 QS, a 2.68 ERA, a 0.96 WHIP, and Will Warren (17 K, 1.69 ERA) — this pitching staff is the best in the league and Kumar Rocker adds even more depth [UP]
4. **Athletic Park Dragons** | Aaron Judge (5 HR, 7 RBI), Bailey Ober (17 K), and a 3.01 ERA with 5 QS in a clean 5-2 win shows the rotation is finally healthy enough to complement the elite offense [UP]
5. **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** | Eight quality starts is a league record-threatening number; Paul Skenes (0.90 ERA, 0.50 WHIP), Imanaga, and Valdez make this the deepest rotation in the game, even if the offense remains quiet [UP]
6. **Albuquerque Bombers** | Byron Buxton (3 HR, 8 R) and Ivan Herrera (2 HR) fueled a 5-4 win; aggressive waiver activity keeps plugging holes, and a Week 5 matchup with the struggling Reapers is a golden opportunity [SAME]
7. **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** | José Ramírez (4 HR, 5 SB, .533 OBP) and Yordan Alvarez (4 HR, 7 RBI) are as good a 1-2 punch as any in the league; the -0.61 luck delta is a real concern but the talent is undeniable [UP]
8. **North Correa** | Nico Hoerner (11 RBI) and a 5 QS week show real depth, but a 5.56 ERA and only 2 category wins in the matchup confirm this team needs pitching help badly despite the Ohtani and stolen-base upside [SAME]
9. **Hiroshima Radiation** | Skubal and Eovaldi both posted sub-1.50 ERAs and the team ERA was a stellar 2.81, yet they lost 6-4 — the -1 NSVH is a killer and the offense's 6 HR and 1 SB simply aren't enough firepower [DOWN]
10. **Riverside Roughnecks** | A 4-4 tie feels like a missed opportunity against a weak Wombats squad, but Misiorowski (14 K) and a solid bullpen show real pitching upside; the offense needs more punch than Xavier Edwards walks can provide [SAME]
11. **Canberra Wombats** | Jo Adell (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Pete Crow-Armstrong (5 R) gave the offense life in a tie, and 4 category wins is an improvement — but a .283 OBP and 49.2 IP are troublingly low roster-utilization numbers [UP]
12. **Buttville Skidmarks** | Junior Caminero (3 HR) and Bryan Reynolds (.500 OBP, 7 RBI) are legitimate weapons, but MacKenzie Gore's 6.52 ERA and Gausman's 1.55 WHIP continue to doom this team in the pitching categories [SAME]
13. **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** | Losing 7-2 to the hottest team in the league stings less than the underlying numbers — 33 hits, a .257 OBP, and only 2 category wins confirm this offense is broken, and dropping McGreevy without a real replacement is worrying [SAME]
14. **Benchwarmers** | Michael King's 0.82 ERA was the lone bright spot in a 2-8 shellacking; with 4 HR, 45 K, and a +1.21 luck delta masking how bad things really are, this team is one average week away from a genuine crisis [DOWN]

---

## Waiver Wire

No notable moves this week — which, honestly, is its own kind of news. When the hottest team in the league just set two single-week records and the trade value section has a pitcher with a 6.52 ERA on the block, the fact that nobody is making aggressive waiver moves feels like the whole league is standing still while **BaseballPro77** is already halfway down the road. Somebody call their shot. Week 5 isn't going to fix itself.
